text: The Kaplans described four sequential components of a restorative environment: being away, extent, fascination, and compatibility. Each is necessary but insufficient alone. Being away involves psychological distance — a sense of departure from ordinary demands, achievable even in a city park. Extent refers to an environment rich enough to constitute a whole other world, requiring no supplementary mental effort to populate. Fascination and compatibility complete the picture, but it is extent that researchers find most underappreciated: a narrow strip of urban landscaping fails to restore not because it lacks beauty but because its shallow scope forces the mind to reach beyond it, reimposing cognitive load.
question: Why does a narrow strip of urban landscaping fail to restore attention according to the Kaplans' model?

options.text: It lacks the fractal complexity needed to engage involuntary attention
Its shallow extent forces the mind beyond it, reimposing cognitive load
It creates compatibility conflicts by juxtaposing natural and urban elements
It provides being away but fails to generate the fascination component
